log:
# Change log — Integrative Biology of Type 2 Diabetes

## 2026-06-27

Ingested the review article **"The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes"** (Roden & Shulman,
*Nature* 576:51-60, 2019; DOI:10.1038/s41586-019-1797-8) into a fresh BioOKF v0.5 bundle.

- Saved the source unchanged to `raw/source.md`.
- Created 2 source/provenance nodes: 1 ingested `Publication` (the review, anchored to
  `raw/source.md`) and 1 external-reference `Study` (the Mahajan 2018 T2D coding-variant GWAS,
  cited not ingested).
- Authored 25 typed concept docs spanning 11 node types: Disease (3), Phenotype (3), Anatomy (4),
  CellType (1), Molecule (5), BiologicalPathway (3), Gene (4), Exposure (2), MethodOrProcedure (1),
  Organism (1).
- Added 92 provenance-stamped edges. Key claims captured: insulin resistance as the common
  abnormality of obesity and T2D; the plasma-membrane sn-1,2-DAG -> PKCepsilon -> insulin-receptor
  (Thr1160) mechanism of hepatic insulin resistance; acetyl-CoA allosteric activation of
  gluconeogenesis driving fasting hyperglycaemia; NEFA flux from insulin-resistant WAT driving
  NAFLD; the role of pancreatic beta-cell compensation/failure; gene variants (TCF7L2, TM6SF2,
  NAT2, TBC1D4); a single saturated-fat load associated with ~70% higher hepatic gluconeogenesis;
  exercise reversing insulin resistance via GLUT4; and the Astyanax mexicanus cavefish IR-mutation
  model of adaptive insulin resistance.
- Lint: 0 errors, 0 warnings (every edge object resolves to a created node).
name: Type 2 diabetes

13C magnetic resonance spectroscopy.body:
# 13C magnetic resonance spectroscopy

Direct assessment of glycogen synthesis by 13C MRS demonstrated lower rates of postprandial and
insulin-regulated hepatic glycogen synthesis in people with T2D, and quantified increased hepatic
[gluconeogenesis](../biologicalpathway/hepatic-gluconeogenesis.md). Combined with 31P MRS it
identified impaired insulin-stimulated muscle glycogen synthesis as the major factor in muscle
[insulin resistance](../phenotype/insulin-resistance.md).
13C magnetic resonance spectroscopy.description: Non-invasive spectroscopic technique used to quantify hepatic glycogen synthesis, gluconeogenesis and muscle glycogen synthesis in vivo, central to the human metabolic-flux studies summarized by the review.

Astyanax mexicanus.body:
# Astyanax mexicanus

The blind cave fish endures infrequent, limited nutrient availability and develops
hyperglycaemia, steatosis and [insulin resistance](../phenotype/insulin-resistance.md) owing to a
mutation in its IR gene, similar to that seen in some patients with Rabson-Mendenhall syndrome.
Despite abnormal energy metabolism it shows delayed senescence, supporting a survival benefit of
limited insulin-dependent glucose disposal.
Astyanax mexicanus.description: Blind cave fish that, owing to a mutation in its insulin receptor gene, develops hyperglycaemia, steatosis and insulin resistance as an adaptation to a nutrient-limited environment, illustrating a survival benefit of insulin resistance.

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

edges.object: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

De novo lipogenesis.body:
# De novo lipogenesis

Hepatic DNL is activated transcriptionally via SREBP1c, ChREBP, mTORC1-SREBP1c and
fructose-stimulated pathways, not exclusively via IR kinase activity. The review notes that NEFA
re-esterification, rather than DNL, probably accounts for the majority of hepatic lipogenesis and
VLDL secretion, contributing to [hepatic steatosis](../phenotype/hepatic-steatosis.md).
De novo lipogenesis.description: Synthesis of fatty acids from carbohydrate-derived precursors; hepatic DNL is driven by insulin (via SREBP1c), ChREBP, mTORC1 and fructose, contributing to hepatic TAG accumulation and NAFLD.

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

Diacylglycerol.body:
# Diacylglycerol

In obese humans with NAFLD, the plasma-membrane *sn*-1,2-DAG-PKCepsilon pathway tightly
correlates with hepatic [insulin resistance](../phenotype/insulin-resistance.md). DAG triggers
translocation of [PKCepsilon](../molecule/protein-kinase-c-epsilon.md) (liver) and PKCtheta
(muscle) to the plasma membrane, inducing inhibitory phosphorylation of the insulin receptor and
IRS-1.
Diacylglycerol.description: Lipid second messenger; plasma-membrane sn-1,2-DAG accumulation from lipid overload activates novel PKC isoforms, the proximal trigger of hepatic and muscle insulin resistance.

GLUT4.body:
# GLUT4

Inhibition of AS160 (TBC1D4) threonine phosphorylation and RAC1 activation accelerates
trafficking of GLUT4-containing storage vesicles to the plasma membrane, enabling insulin-
stimulated glucose uptake. A single bout of exercise promotes GLUT4 translocation and glucose
uptake independently of insulin via AMP kinase.
GLUT4.description: Insulin-regulated glucose transporter; insulin-stimulated trafficking of GLUT4-containing storage vesicles to the plasma membrane enables glucose uptake in skeletal muscle and adipose tissue.

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

edges.object: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

Hepatic gluconeogenesis.body:
# Hepatic gluconeogenesis

Increased hepatic acetyl-CoA allosterically stimulates pyruvate carboxylase flux, which along
with increased glycerol flux maintains gluconeogenesis and endogenous glucose production. A single
oral saturated fat load was associated with 70% higher rates of hepatic gluconeogenesis. Fasting
[hyperglycaemia](../phenotype/hyperglycaemia.md) in T2D results from increased gluconeogenesis
plus hepatic insulin resistance.
Hepatic gluconeogenesis.description: Hepatic synthesis of glucose from non-carbohydrate precursors; increased rates, driven by acetyl-CoA allosteric activation of pyruvate carboxylase and glycerol flux, cause fasting hyperglycaemia in T2D.

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

Hepatic steatosis.body:
# Hepatic steatosis

The morphologic accumulation of [triacylglycerol/DAG](../molecule/diacylglycerol.md) in the
liver. The review notes that NEFA re-esterification (rather than de novo lipogenesis) probably
accounts for the majority of hepatic lipogenesis and VLDL secretion, and that weight loss from
very-low-calorie diets rapidly normalizes hepatic steatosis and hepatic insulin resistance.
Hepatic steatosis.description: Accumulation of triacylglycerol within hepatocytes; the morphologic feature underlying NAFLD, driven mainly by NEFA flux from insulin-resistant white adipose tissue and de novo lipogenesis.

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

Hyperglycaemia.body:
# Hyperglycaemia

Fasting hyperglycaemia in T2D results from increased rates of
[hepatic gluconeogenesis](../biologicalpathway/hepatic-gluconeogenesis.md) and endogenous glucose
production, together with hepatic insulin resistance that reduces insulin's ability to suppress
glucose output.
Hyperglycaemia.description: Elevated plasma glucose concentration; in T2D, fasting hyperglycaemia results from increased hepatic gluconeogenesis and endogenous glucose production combined with hepatic insulin resistance.

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

edges.object: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

Insulin.body:
# Insulin

Under postprandial conditions, insulin rapidly stimulates lipid storage by inhibiting
[lipolysis](../biologicalpathway/wat-lipolysis.md), suppresses
[gluconeogenesis](../biologicalpathway/hepatic-gluconeogenesis.md) (indirectly via reduced WAT
NEFA/glycerol flux), and stimulates glycogen synthesis. Reduced insulin action defines
[insulin resistance](../phenotype/insulin-resistance.md).
Insulin.description: Anabolic peptide hormone secreted by pancreatic beta-cells; stimulates glucose uptake and glycogen synthesis, suppresses lipolysis and hepatic gluconeogenesis. Its reduced action is insulin resistance.

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

edges.object: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

Insulin resistance.body:
# Insulin resistance

The common underlying abnormality in obesity and T2D. In the liver and muscle, plasma-membrane
*sn*-1,2-[diacylglycerol](../molecule/diacylglycerol.md) accumulation triggers translocation of
novel PKC isoforms ([PKCepsilon](../molecule/protein-kinase-c-epsilon.md) in liver; PKCtheta and
PKCepsilon in muscle), which phosphorylate and inhibit the insulin receptor / IRS-1. Skeletal
muscle insulin resistance, due to impaired insulin-stimulated glucose transport and glycogen
synthesis, is one of the earliest pathogenic events, declining decades before T2D onset.
Insulin resistance.description: Reduced ability of insulin to stimulate glucose disposal and suppress hepatic glucose production; the common underlying abnormality in obesity and T2D, present in liver, skeletal muscle and white adipose tissue.

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

Leptin.body:
# Leptin

A fuel gauge for energy stored as TAG in WAT and glycogen in liver. The early postabsorptive
decline in hepatic glycogenolysis lowers plasma insulin and glucose, producing roughly a 50%
reduction in plasma leptin; a fall below 1 ng/ml stimulates the HPA axis, raising corticosterone
that drives WAT lipolysis and maintains
[gluconeogenesis](../biologicalpathway/hepatic-gluconeogenesis.md) during starvation.
Leptin.description: Adipocyte-derived hormone reflecting white-adipose-tissue mass; acts as a fuel gauge signalling energy depletion to the brain and driving the leptin-HPA axis in the fed-to-fasting transition.

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

edges.object: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

Liver.body:
# Liver

Increased fatty acid flux to the liver raises hepatic beta-oxidation and acetyl-CoA content,
which allosterically activates pyruvate carboxylase flux and maintains
[gluconeogenesis](../biologicalpathway/hepatic-gluconeogenesis.md). Hepatic
[DAG](../molecule/diacylglycerol.md) accumulation triggers
[PKCepsilon](../molecule/protein-kinase-c-epsilon.md) translocation and Thr1160 phosphorylation
of the insulin receptor, causing hepatic insulin resistance.
Liver.description: Central organ of glucose and lipid metabolism; site of gluconeogenesis, glycogen synthesis, de novo lipogenesis and ectopic lipid (TAG/DAG) accumulation in insulin resistance.

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

NAT2 (gene).body:
# NAT2 (gene)

A European GWAS reported that a nonsynonymous *NAT2* variant is associated with insulin
resistance, decreased adipocyte differentiation, decreased insulin-mediated glucose uptake and
increased WAT lipolysis. Silencing the mouse orthologue NAT1 induces
[insulin resistance](../phenotype/insulin-resistance.md), glucose intolerance, ectopic TAG/DAG
accumulation and activation of hepatic PKCepsilon and muscle PKCtheta.
NAT2 (gene).description: Gene whose nonsynonymous variant a European GWAS associated with insulin resistance and related traits; the mouse orthologue NAT1 links reduced mitochondrial function to ectopic lipid and nPKC-driven insulin resistance.

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

edges.object: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease.body:
# Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease

Defined by hepatic [triacylglycerol](../molecule/diacylglycerol.md) accumulation. The review
argues NAFLD develops chiefly through increased NEFA flux from insulin-resistant white adipose
tissue rather than requiring selective hepatic insulin resistance. The
*sn*-1,2-DAG-PKCepsilon pathway tightly correlates with hepatic insulin resistance in obese
humans with NAFLD.
Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease.description: Liver disease defined by hepatic triacylglycerol accumulation, spanning simple steatosis to non-alcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH); tightly linked to hepatic insulin resistance in obesity and T2D.

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

edges.object: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

Obesity disorder.body:
# Obesity disorder

Excess adiposity drives [white adipose tissue](../anatomy/white-adipose-tissue.md) dysfunction,
relative hypoxia and macrophage infiltration, which inhibit adipocyte insulin sensitivity and
increase lipolysis and NEFA flux to the liver, promoting hepatic
[insulin resistance](../phenotype/insulin-resistance.md) and
[NAFLD](nafld.md).
Obesity disorder.description: Metabolic disorder of excess adiposity; the most frequent metabolic disorder alongside T2D and a driver of white-adipose-tissue dysfunction and insulin resistance.

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

edges.object: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

Pancreatic beta-cell.body:
# Pancreatic beta-cell

Secretes [insulin](../molecule/insulin.md) in response to glucose. Compensatory
hyperinsulinaemia promotes hepatic de novo lipogenesis, steatosis and WAT expansion; beta-cell
function then declines (partly glucolipotoxicity) just before T2D onset, with population-specific
differences (faster decline in African Americans).
Pancreatic beta-cell.description: Insulin-secreting endocrine cell of the pancreatic islet; compensatory hypersecretion followed by declining function (glucolipotoxicity) underlies the transition to overt T2D.

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

edges.object: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

Pancreatic islet.body:
# Pancreatic islet

The review attributes most of the 400+ T2D-associated gene variants to islet function. Initially,
[beta-cells](../celltype/pancreatic-beta-cell.md) compensate for insulin resistance by secreting
more insulin (hyperinsulinaemia); declining beta-cell function, partly due to glucolipotoxicity,
typically occurs just before T2D onset.
Pancreatic islet.description: Endocrine micro-organ of the pancreas containing insulin-secreting beta-cells; deficient insulin secretion in the context of insulin resistance defines T2D.

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

edges.object: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

Physical exercise.body:
# Physical exercise

A single bout of exercise activates AMP kinase, promotes translocation of
[GLUT4](../molecule/glut4.md) and glucose uptake independently of insulin, and completely
reversed the metabolic abnormalities (diversion of carbohydrate to hepatic DNL,
hypertriglyceridaemia) seen in insulin-resistant first-degree relatives of T2D patients.
Physical exercise.description: Behavioral exposure; a single bout of exercise activates AMP kinase and promotes insulin-independent GLUT4 translocation and glucose uptake, reversing diet-induced metabolic abnormalities.

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

edges.object: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

Protein kinase C epsilon.body:
# Protein kinase C epsilon

Translocated to the plasma membrane by [DAG](diacylglycerol.md), where it binds and
phosphorylates Thr1160 of the insulin receptor, inhibiting IR kinase activity. The high
evolutionary conservation of the Thr1160 residue suggests the DAG-nPKC mechanism served a
survival role during starvation while favouring
[insulin resistance](../phenotype/insulin-resistance.md) during overnutrition.
Protein kinase C epsilon.description: Novel (calcium-independent) protein kinase C isoform; activated by plasma-membrane sn-1,2-DAG, it translocates and phosphorylates Thr1160 of the insulin receptor, inhibiting IR kinase activity (hepatic insulin resistance).

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

edges.object: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

synonyms: novel protein kinase C isoform epsilon

Saturated fat intake.body:
# Saturated fat intake

A single oral saturated-fat load simultaneously induced
[insulin resistance](../phenotype/insulin-resistance.md) in liver, skeletal muscle and WAT, and
was associated with 70% higher rates of hepatic
[gluconeogenesis](../biologicalpathway/hepatic-gluconeogenesis.md) and 20% lower rates of net
hepatic glycogenolysis. Chronic overfeeding also raised intestinal endotoxins promoting
TLR4-induced cytokine release by Kupffer cells.
Saturated fat intake.description: Dietary exposure to saturated fat; a single oral saturated-fat load acutely induces insulin resistance in liver, skeletal muscle and white adipose tissue in healthy humans.

Skeletal muscle.body:
# Skeletal muscle

13C and 31P magnetic resonance spectroscopy identified impaired insulin-stimulated glycogen
synthesis as the major factor responsible for muscle
[insulin resistance](../phenotype/insulin-resistance.md), with reduced insulin-stimulated
[GLUT4](../molecule/glut4.md)-mediated glucose transport as the rate-controlling step. A single
bout of exercise activates AMP kinase and promotes GLUT4 translocation independently of insulin.
Skeletal muscle.description: Major site of insulin-stimulated glucose disposal; impaired insulin-stimulated glucose transport and glycogen synthesis here is the primary early defect in T2D pathogenesis.

T2D coding-variant fine-mapping GWAS (Ma.body:
# T2D coding-variant fine-mapping GWAS (Mahajan 2018)

External-reference **source node** (`Study`, `subtype: gwas`), cited by the review (reference 2).
Its `xref` records the DOI; it has no `raw_source` because the GWAS itself was not ingested.
Claims attributed to the GWAS set `primary_source: T2D coding-variant fine-mapping GWAS (Mahajan
2018)`.
T2D coding-variant fine-mapping GWAS (Ma.description: Genome-wide association study fine-mapping more than 400 T2D-associated variants, cited by the review as the basis for the heritability and islet-function claims.

edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

TBC1D4 (gene).body:
# TBC1D4 (gene)

A common Greenlandic *TBC1D4* (AS160) variant confers muscle insulin resistance, postprandial
hyperinsulinaemia, hyperglycaemia and type 2 diabetes. AS160 links insulin signalling to
[GLUT4](../molecule/glut4.md) trafficking and glucose transport.
TBC1D4 (gene).description: Gene encoding AS160, a Rab-GAP linking insulin signalling to GLUT4 trafficking; a common Greenlandic variant confers muscle insulin resistance and type 2 diabetes.

TCF7L2 (gene).body:
# TCF7L2 (gene)

In unbiased cluster analyses of recent-onset diabetes, a *TCF7L2* variant related to insulin
deficiency. Most of the 400+ T2D-associated variants relate to islet function but explain less
than 20% of overall [T2D](../disease/type-2-diabetes.md) risk.
TCF7L2 (gene).description: Gene whose common variant is the strongest single known genetic risk factor for type 2 diabetes; in the review's cluster analysis a TCF7L2 variant related to the insulin-deficient subgroup.

TM6SF2 (gene).body:
# TM6SF2 (gene)

A *TM6SF2* variant related to the severely insulin-resistant diabetes cluster predicted
nephropathy, cardiovascular disease and [NAFLD](../disease/nafld.md) during a 5-year follow-up.
TM6SF2 (gene).description: Gene whose variant, in the review's cluster analysis, relates to the severely insulin-resistant diabetes subgroup and predicts NAFLD.

The integrative biology of type 2 diabet.body:
# The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

Ingested-document **source node** (`Publication`, `subtype: article`) anchored to `raw/source.md`
via `raw_source`. This is the review article being ingested. Every claim extracted from the
article carries `primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman
2019)` and a `reported_in` edge back to this node.

Roden, M. & Shulman, G. I. The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes. *Nature* **576**, 51-60
(2019). DOI:10.1038/s41586-019-1797-8.
The integrative biology of type 2 diabet.description: Nature Review (volume 576, pages 51-60, 2019) by Michael Roden and Gerald I. Shulman summarizing the integrative biology of insulin resistance and hepatic gluconeogenesis in obesity and type 2 diabetes.

edges.object: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)
edges.primary_source: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

The integrative biology of type 2 diabet.identifier: The integrative biology of type 2 diabetes (Roden & Shulman 2019)

Type 2 diabetes.body:
# Type 2 diabetes

A heterogeneous disease characterized by deficient insulin secretion by pancreatic islet
beta-cells in the context of [insulin resistance](../phenotype/insulin-resistance.md). A GWAS
found more than 400 T2D-associated variants, mostly related to islet function, but the individual
genes explain less than 20% of overall disease risk. Most of the epidemic is driven by acquired
alterations, as shown by lifestyle-modification studies demonstrating T2D remission.
Type 2 diabetes.description: Heterogeneous metabolic disease characterized by deficient insulin secretion by pancreatic islet beta-cells in the context of insulin resistance, leading to chronic hyperglycaemia.

White adipose tissue.body:
# White adipose tissue

Dysfunction of WAT and circulating metabolites initially modulate tissue communication and
insulin signalling. In insulin-resistant states, adipocyte dysfunction (relative hypoxia, HIF-1a
activation, macrophage infiltration) increases [lipolysis](../biologicalpathway/wat-lipolysis.md)
and NEFA/glycerol flux to the liver. [Leptin](../molecule/leptin.md) released from WAT signals
energy stores to the brain.
White adipose tissue.description: Lipid-storing tissue that, when dysfunctional in obesity and T2D, drives lipolysis and NEFA/glycerol flux to the liver, modulating tissue communication and insulin signalling.

White adipose tissue lipolysis.body:
# White adipose tissue lipolysis

Insulin inhibits lipolysis via ATGL/CGI-58, PDE3B and PKA-controlled hormone-sensitive lipase and
perilipins. In insulin-resistant states, immune-cell infiltration of dysfunctional
[WAT](../anatomy/white-adipose-tissue.md) increases lipolysis and NEFA/glycerol flux to the
[liver](../anatomy/liver.md), aggravating hepatic
[insulin resistance](../phenotype/insulin-resistance.md) and NAFLD.
White adipose tissue lipolysis.description: Hydrolysis of stored triacylglycerol in white adipose tissue releasing NEFA and glycerol; suppressed by insulin and stimulated by glucocorticoids, its dysregulation drives hepatic insulin resistance.
